section.heading: Reports to Congress: submission in electronic form

subsection.heading: Requirement.—
subsection.content: Whenever the Secretary of Defense or any other official of the Department of Defense submits to Congress (or any committee of either House of Congress) a report that the Secretary (or other official) is required by law to submit, the Secretary (or other official) shall provide to Congress (or such committee) a copy of the report in an electronic medium.

subsection.heading: Exception.—
subsection.content: Subsection (a) does not apply to a report submitted in classified form.

subsection.heading: Definition.—
subsection.content: In this section, the term “report” includes any certification, notification, or other communication in writing.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 107–107, div. A, title X, § 1042(a), Dec. 28, 2001, 115 Stat. 1218; amended Pub. L. 107–314, div. A, title X, § 1042, Dec. 2, 2002, 116 Stat. 2646.)

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2002—Subsec. (a). Pub. L. 107–314 substituted “shall provide to Congress (or” for “shall, upon request by any committee of Congress to which the report is submitted or referred, provide to Congress (or each”.
